Managed services + reinvention offset consulting cyclicality; mid-teens margins hold

open_questions: Mechanical **accumulate** on high filing FCF yield; confirm whether FY2025 OCF is sustainable post-AI or should be normalized lower in base case.
Business optimization ($615M FY2025, $308M H1 FY2026): one-time vs recurring efficiency program?
Proxy compensation alignment (Julie Sweet PEO tags): full-tier DEF 14A review.
No approved third-party sources; re-scan when Substacks or fund letters added.
thesis: Accenture is the world's largest professional-services firm by revenue, helping Global 2000 companies and governments with strategy, technology integration, and managed operations. **[Fact]** FY2025 revenue was **$69.7 billion** (+7% local currency), split roughly half consulting and half managed services. **[Fact]** The company employs about **779,000** people and serves **9,000+** clients, including most of the Fortune Global 100. **[Fact]** Cash generation is strong: operating cash flow **$11.5 billion** minus capital spending **$0.6 billion** in FY2025. **[Inference]** Accenture earns by deploying skilled labor and proprietary methods at scale, with increasing mix shift toward recurring managed-services contracts.
why_market_wrong: The stock trades at a depressed multiple versus historical quality-compounder levels while filing cash flow remains robust. **[Inference]** The market may be overweighting **generative AI disruption** (clients need less consulting labor) and underweighting Accenture's **$3 billion Gen AI investment** and reinvention positioning. **[Fact]** FY2025 bookings were **$80.6 billion**, down 1% in local currency, but Q2 FY2026 bookings rebounded to **$22.1 billion** (+1% local currency). **[Inference]** No dated HK predictive attribute exists; the edge is **quality franchise at a trough free-cash-flow yield** (~12% on FY2025 owner cash), not a catalyst with a known payoff date.
